summary: Gate rc=0 but Phase 1 acceptance + Decision Lock are NOT satisfied: pytest did not actually run (missing pytest + gate not fail-closed), Langfuse is unreachable and treated optional, Deal API wiring uses /api/deals instead of locked /deals, checkpoint encryption is not applied to persisted checkpoints, PII canary is simulated/does not scan DB, and transition_deal is not approval-enforced (only probed). Recurring from cycles 1–3 ⇒ likely stuck loop.

blockers.title: Phase1 gate is not fail-closed (pytest can PASS while failing/missing)
blockers.details: `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` runs pytest via a pipeline (`python3 -m pytest ... | tee ... | tail -30`) without `set -o pipefail`, so the pipeline exit status is from `tail`, not pytest. It also marks pytest as optional (`required=false`). In the provided gate output, `/usr/bin/python3: No module named pytest` appears, yet the gate records pytest=PASSED and overall `PHASE1_GATE=PASSED`.
repro_steps: Observe the 'No module named pytest' error while pytest is still marked PASSED
blockers.expected: Gate fails (nonzero) if pytest cannot run or any tests fail, per acceptance criteria.
blockers.actual: Gate exits 0 and prints PHASE1_GATE=PASSED even when pytest is missing/failing.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make pytest required and enforce pipeline failure propagation (e.g., `set -o pipefail` and/or check `PIPESTATUS[0]`), and run pytest from a known environment (install `.[dev]` or use the repo venv).
validation_steps: Ensure pytest is absent and confirm `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` exits nonzero
Install dev deps and confirm `python3 -m pytest tests/` exits 0 and only then the gate passes

blockers.title: Langfuse is required but unhealthy/unreachable and treated optional (no real trace proof)
blockers.details: Decision Lock requires Langfuse self-hosted on :3001 and health 200. Current artifacts show `gate_artifacts/phase1/langfuse_health.json` with `healthy=false`/`unreachable`. `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` marks Langfuse optional (`required=false`). The soak harness also treats Langfuse failures as non-critical and can PASS with 100% Langfuse failures. `gate_artifacts/phase1/langfuse_trace_proof.md` is a template and does not contain an actual trace ID or redaction proof.
blockers.expected: Langfuse reachable at :3001; gate fails if health check is not 200; trace proof artifact contains an actual trace reference from a real workflow.
blockers.actual: Langfuse health is unreachable, gate still passes, and trace proof is non-evidence template text.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make Langfuse gate required; ensure Langfuse is started (compose/env) and reachable at :3001; treat Langfuse failures as failures in soak/gate; generate a real trace and record its ID plus evidence of redaction.
validation_steps: Run `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` and confirm langfuse=PASSED and soak shows langfuse failures=0
Verify `gate_artifacts/phase1/langfuse_trace_proof.md` includes an actual trace ID

blockers.title: Decision-locked Deal API endpoints and Agent API port are not followed
blockers.details: Authoritative roadmap mapping is `GET /deals`, `GET /deals/{id}`, `POST /deals/{id}/transition`. Current implementation and artifacts use `/api/deals*` (probe/client/deal_tools gate artifacts). Decision Lock also fixes Agent API at :8095, but this repo config (Dockerfile/compose/gates) uses :8091 as the backend URL/port.
repro_steps: Inspect Deal API client/probe paths and gate artifacts under gate_artifacts/phase1
Inspect Dockerfile/docker-compose/gates for backend port (8091 vs 8095)
blockers.expected: Deal tools use `/deals*` endpoints and Agent API binds on :8095 per Decision Lock.
blockers.actual: Deal tools/probe/artifacts use `/api/deals*` and backend/gates use :8091.
blockers.recommended_fix: Align Deal API paths to `/deals*` everywhere (probe/client/tests/artifacts) and move Agent API/bindings/gate URLs to :8095 per Decision Lock; if the canonical contract differs, update the locked spec (requires human decision) rather than drifting implementation.
validation_steps: Run the contract probe and confirm required_endpoints paths are `/deals*`
Run the phase1 gate and confirm all health checks target :8095 and pass

blockers.title: Checkpoint encryption is not applied to persisted checkpoints; kill-9 is only simulated
blockers.details: `src/core/hitl/checkpoint.py` persists `checkpoint_data` as plaintext JSON (`json.dumps(checkpoint_data)`), so sensitive content can be stored unencrypted at rest. `src/core/security/checkpoint_encryption.py` provides an encryptor but does not implement the Decision-Locked `SecurePostgresSaver` wrapper. `scripts/gates/kill9_encrypted_test.sh` explicitly states it cannot actually kill a running agent and only simulates encrypt/decrypt in memory, so it does not prove DB-at-rest encryption or real crash recovery.
blockers.expected: Checkpoint persistence is encrypted at rest (per Roadmap §3.6) and kill-9 recovery validates restart/resume from DB-backed encrypted state.
blockers.actual: Checkpoint data is stored plaintext and kill-9 gate validates only in-memory encryption roundtrip.
blockers.recommended_fix: Encrypt/decrypt in the real checkpoint persistence layer (implement `SecurePostgresSaver` if using LangGraph PostgresSaver, or wrap the existing checkpoint store), and update kill-9 gate to start a real workflow, SIGKILL it, restart, and verify resume + ciphertext-at-rest by querying Postgres.
validation_steps: Persist a checkpoint containing a unique marker and verify it is NOT present in DB plaintext
Run a real kill-9 (SIGKILL) + restart and confirm the workflow resumes correctly

blockers.title: PII canary gate does not inject canary and can skip DB scan (fails open)
blockers.details: `scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh` Step 1 is explicitly a simulated injection; it never routes the canary through the agent/logging/tracing pipeline. DB scanning is skipped when `psql` is unavailable (warns and returns success). This cannot prove that the canary would not appear in logs/traces/DB.
repro_steps: Observe 'Simulated injection' and that DB scan can be skipped if psql missing
blockers.expected: Canary is injected via a real workflow/API call and scans of logs + traces + DB are mandatory (gate fails if any scan cannot run).
blockers.actual: No real injection; DB scan can be skipped; gate still returns PASS.
blockers.recommended_fix: Invoke a real request containing the canary (e.g., through the Agent API), then scan structured logs, Langfuse trace exports, and Postgres tables; fail-closed if any surface cannot be scanned or if canary is found.
validation_steps: Run canary gate with DB+Langfuse running and confirm all scans execute
Introduce an intentional canary leak and confirm the gate FAILS

blockers.title: CRITICAL transition_deal is not approval-enforced; gate only performs a probe (422)
blockers.details: Decision Lock requires CRITICAL tools (transition_deal) to be blocked until approval. In `src/core/agent/deal_tools.py`, missing `approval_id` only triggers a warning and the tool still calls the Deal API. The gate artifact `gate_artifacts/phase1/deal_api_transition_deal.json` shows `probe_mode=true` and `http_status=422`, meaning no approved transition was executed.
repro_steps: Call transition_deal_tool with no approval_id in context
blockers.expected: transition_deal cannot execute without approval and the gate validates a successful approved transition (200/201) with an approval_id.
blockers.actual: transition_deal can execute without approval (warn-only) and the gate records only a probe with HTTP 422.
blockers.recommended_fix: Fail-closed: enforce approval in tool execution (ToolRegistry/handler) so the API is never called without approval_id; update the gate to create/approve a HITL approval and then execute a valid transition, producing a success artifact.
validation_steps: Verify no outbound Deal API call occurs when approval_id is missing
Execute an approved transition and confirm artifact shows success=true and http_status 200/201

majors.title: SafeLogger can leak plaintext tool args/results (e.g., new_stage) and tests do not catch it
majors.details: The locked policy requires hash+length only for tool arguments/results. `SafeLogger` decides hashing via field-name heuristics and does not treat `new_stage`/`stage` as sensitive, so short stage strings can be logged in plaintext. `src/core/agent/deal_tools.py` logs `new_stage` as a separate extra field, and `scripts/gates/tests/test_safe_logger.py` only checks for constants/method names (does not assert redaction behavior).
repro_steps: Review SafeLogger _should_hash_value logic for short strings
Run a transition_deal call and inspect structured logs for plaintext stage
majors.expected: All tool args/results (except explicitly allowed opaque IDs) are hashed+length only; tests assert this behavior.
majors.actual: Stage strings can be logged in plaintext and the existing gate test does not validate redaction.
majors.recommended_fix: Adopt an allowlist-only model (hash everything not in SAFE_FIELD_NAMES), explicitly add `stage/new_stage` to sensitive fields, and update tests to assert that logged extras contain only hash+length for tool args/results.
validation_steps: Create a log event with `new_stage='qualified'` and confirm output stores only hash+length
Run the SafeLogger gate and ensure it fails if plaintext tool args/results appear

majors.title: Deal API contract probe is not true auto-discovery and logs misleading endpoint names
majors.details: The probe hardcodes `/api/deals*` rather than discovering the canonical `/deals*` paths from OpenAPI. It also prints `/deals` in console output while calling `/api/deals` in code, and the generated contract mixes `/api/deals` and `/deals/{id}` in different fields. This undermines the contract-probe requirement and can mask endpoint drift.
repro_steps: Inspect printed endpoint strings vs contract JSON required_endpoints
majors.expected: Probe derives/validates endpoint paths from OpenAPI (or probes multiple candidates) and reports consistent, accurate paths matching the locked spec.
majors.actual: Probe is hardcoded to `/api/deals*` and logs inconsistent paths.
majors.recommended_fix: Parse the OpenAPI spec to locate `/deals*` endpoints (or probe both `/deals*` and `/api/deals*` and select the canonical locked mapping), and make printed output + contract JSON consistent.
validation_steps: Run the probe and confirm it reports the same paths it actually calls
Fail the probe if the locked endpoints are not present

minors.title: DeprecationWarning in Deal API probe (datetime.utcnow)
minors.details: Gate output shows a DeprecationWarning because `scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py` uses `datetime.utcnow()`; Python recommends timezone-aware UTC via `datetime.now(timezone.utc)`.
minors.expected: No deprecation warnings during probe runs.
validation_steps: Re-run the probe and confirm no DeprecationWarning is emitted

evidence: gate_output.log: Gate 1 shows '/usr/bin/python3: No module named pytest' but records '✓ pytest: PASSED' and overall rc=0
scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:109 uses a pytest pipeline with `tail -30` and marks pytest required=false
gate_artifacts/phase1/deal_api_list_deals.json api_endpoint='GET /api/deals' (not `/deals`) and deal_api_contract.json required_endpoints paths are `/api/deals*`
scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:121-126 states simulated injection; :85-88 skips DB scan if psql missing
src/core/agent/deal_tools.py:289-307 warns on missing approval_id but still calls transition_deal
